title: Marketing + Social Media
description:
Type: Part-time to start (20 hrs/week), can grow to full-time



Schedule: Flexible daily hours + one weekly/every other week 1hr meeting during US Mountain Time hours



What this job is: You own our marketing execution. Your job is to generate more booked patients at a lower cost by running and improving google/Meta/Local ads, producing weekly content, and keeping our Wix website updated for offers and SEO. This is not a “post and hope” role. It’s a marketing and creative role with weekly accountability.



What you will do (weekly)

Run our paid ads (Google,
/Instagram, Microsoft/ETC)

Update campaigns, budgets, keywords/audiences, and ad copy

Launch monthly split tests (new offers, hooks, creatives, targeting)

Improve results over time: cost per booked patient

Create and post content (Static you create it yourself, Videos + others content will be provided for editing)

Create and publish weekly social content (posts + short videos)

Maintain our social accounts with consistent, brand-aligned content (shorts from videos provided etc)

Create marketing materials (you design it yourself)

Flyers, promo graphics, patient education visuals, simple videos

Assets used online and inside the office



Update/maintain our Wix website + SEO

Add/update offers, service pages, events, and content

Improve on-page SEO basics (titles, headers, internal links, meta descriptions)



Weekly reporting + meeting

Send a short weekly report before the meeting:

What you changed

What worked / what didn’t

What you’re testing next

New creative shipped this week



Who will thrive in this role

This role is for someone who:

Works without being constantly told what to do (Will give you the goals you need to be capable of knowing how to get there)

Ships creative every week (not “waiting on direction”)

Thinks in experiments and numbers (tests ? results ? next test)

Is consistent and organized (checklists, tracking, follow-through)

Can take feedback fast and improve without ego



Minimum requirements:

You have personally managed Google Ads and Meta 
/Instagram) Ads (not just “assisted”)

You can create your own creatives: Canva (required) + basic video editing (CapCut or similar)

You can write clear marketing copy (hooks, offers, CTAs)

You can edit/update a Wix website and handle basic on-page SEO

You can deliver clean weekly reports (shared Excel sheet) and attend one weekly meeting in Mountain Time



What to submit when applying:

Portfolio link with:

2+ ad creatives you made

6+ social posts you made

2 short videos you made/edited



Two short examples of accounts/campaigns you managed with numbers:

Spend range, goal, what you changed, result (CPL or cost/booked)



Your hourly rate and weekly availability

Confirmation you can attend a weekly meeting in US Mountain Time



How we hire

Shortlisted candidates will complete a paid 2–4 hour skills project (ads + creative).



Pre-interview questions 

What were the last 2 campaigns you personally managed (Google or Other)? Share the goal, what you changed, and the result with numbers.

Share your portfolio link (ads + posts + videos). Which 3 pieces are you most proud of and why?

title: Controller
description:
Looking for someone with Controller and FP&A experience



The Controller will be a pivotal senior finance leader, responsible for overseeing all accounting operations, ensuring the integrity of financial data, and providing strategic financial insights to management. This role demands a strong understanding of the staffing industry's unique financial dynamics, including payroll complexities and cost allocation, to support continued growth and operational efficiency.



Essential Duties and Responsibilities

Financial Reporting & General Ledger: Oversee the month-end and year-end close processes and the preparation of accurate, timely internal and external financial statements, ensuring compliance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP).

Budgeting & Forecasting: Manage the annual budgeting and forecasting processes, including cash flow analysis and variance reporting, to help department heads and executives make informed strategic decisions.

Compliance & Audits: Develop, implement, and monitor robust internal controls to safeguard company assets and ensure compliance with all federal, state, and local tax laws and regulations. Coordinate all internal and external audit activities.

title: Part-time Graphic Designer & AI Creative Specialist – Content, Design & Video
description:
Summary

Join the team behind Australia's Cybersecurity Business of the Year 2024 and Cybersecurity Entrepreneur of the Year 2025, and we need a creative person who thinks in AI, is creative, moves fast, and builds brands that get noticed. If that's YOU, we want you to read further.



Company: Cyber Ethos, Australia

Engagement: Remote | Part-Time Contractor / Service Partnership Agreement

Hours: 15 hours per week



About the Role

Cyber Ethos is a leading cybersecurity advisory firm helping boards, CEOs, and executives understand and govern cyber risk. We are looking for a creative powerhouse who thinks in systems, not tasks. This is not a traditional design role. You will be the engine behind our brand visibility across LinkedIn, Meta, X/Twitter, and our website, using AI tools to produce premium content at scale, fast and with impact.



The value you bring is creative judgement and storytelling, not hours spent on repetitive production.



If you are still manually doing everything by hand, this role is not for you.



What You Will Do / Key Responsibilities

1) Design scroll-stopping, on-brand thumbnails for video content, blog posts, and social media campaigns across all platforms

?2) Repurpose long-form video content (webinars, keynotes, interviews) into short-form clips optimised for LinkedIn, Instagram Reels, Meta, and X using AI-assisted editing

?3) Create AI-generated video content using text-to-video tools, bringing thought leadership topics to life visually without traditional production overhead

?4) Build and maintain a content calendar that ensures consistent brand presence across all channels. You will be working very closely with the Digital Marketing and Executive Support Officer.

5) Write and format LinkedIn carousel posts, blog headers, social media graphics, and website visual assets aligned to Dr. Kiran Kewalramani's personal brand and Cyber Ethos positioning

6) Monitor content performance, iterate on creative based on analytics, and proactively suggest new formats and campaign ideas

7) Own the brand visual identity across all touchpoints, ensuring consistency in tone, colour, typography, and message



Role Requirements

1) Minimum 2-3 years of experience in social media content creation or digital design, with a strong portfolio demonstrating AI-assisted creative work

2) Proven track record of growing professional brand presence on LinkedIn specifically

3) Some familiarity with the cybersecurity, technology, or professional services sector is a strong advantage

4) Ability to work autonomously, manage deadlines, and bring ideas without being asked

5) Strong written English for captions, blog intros, and social copy

6) Proactive mindset: you bring ideas to the table, not just execution



AI Toolkit Requirements

This is non-negotiable. You must be proficient (not curious about, but proficient in) using AI toolkit. Some examples (but not limited to) of AI toolkit is provided below. Cyber Ethos may consider getting the subscription for the recommended toolkit



Visual Design & Thumbnails

1) Canva AI / Magic Studio for thumbnails, carousels, social graphics, and brand templates

2) Adobe Firefly + Adobe Express for professional-grade AI image generation and asset creation

?3) Opusclip

4) Midjourney V7 for photorealistic and concept-level brand visuals

?5) DALL-E 3 / ChatGPT image generation for rapid ideation and custom visual prompts



Text-to-Video & Video Production

1) HeyGen for AI avatar-based video content and spokesperson-style videos

?2) Runway ML (Gen-3) for video editing, motion graphics, and scene generation

?3) InVideo AI for converting blog content and scripts into polished short-form videos

?4) Pictory AI for automatic video repurposing from long-form content

?5) Kling AI or Google Veo 3 for cinematic text-to-video generation

6) CapCut AI or Opus clip for short-form video trimming, captioning, and platform-ready formatting

?

Copywriting & Content Intelligence

1) ChatGPT-4o / Claude for caption writing, blog drafts, headline generation, and repurposing briefs

?2) Jasper AI for social media copy and long-form content aligned to brand voice

?3) Perplexity AI for real-time research to keep content accurate and timely



SEO & Performance

1) VidIQ for YouTube and video thumbnail optimisation and SEO tagging

2) Surfer SEO for blog content optimisation and keyword-led structure

?

Scheduling & Distribution

1) Buffer AI or Hootsuite OwlyWriter AI for automated scheduling and cross-platform publishing

?2) LinkedIn Creator tools natively for analytics, newsletter, and post optimisation



Voiceover & Audio

1) ElevenLabs for AI voiceovers on video content without requiring a recording studio

?2) Beatoven AI for AI-generated background music in video content



Requirements

• Proven experience in digital and social media design

• Strong portfolio of marketing or social media assets

• Excellent English communication skills

• Ability to work independently and meet deadlines

• Proficiency in tools such as Adobe Creative Suite, Canva, and Figma/Adobe XD

• Experience with marketing platforms like GoHighLevel is a plus



What Success Looks Like

Within 90 days, the right person will have built a repeatable AI-powered content production system where a single keynote talk, blog post, or podcast episode is automatically transformed into at least 8 to 10 pieces of platform-optimised content, scheduled, published, and tracked, with minimal manual intervention.



How to Apply

1) Submit your application immediately.

2) In your application provide at least 3 examples of AI-generated or AI-assisted content you have created, including at least one short-form video and one social media campaign.

3) Tell us which AI tools you use daily and what your workflow looks like for taking a raw idea from brief to published content.

4) Interviews will be scheduled in March 2026, with the right candidate starting immediately.



Performance Expectations

• Deliver all assigned tasks on time

• Maintain high brand compliance

• Typical output: 7–12 assets per week

• Minimal revisions and proactive creative suggestions

* Attend Weekly meetings

title: Elite Web Designer - Futuristic, Animation-Heavy Landing Pages (GSAP, Three.js, WebGL)
description:
We are building a landing page for an AI-powered operator agent platform, and we need someone who builds websites that make people stop scrolling and say "what the hell was that."



This is NOT a typical landing page job. We are looking for someone who creates web experiences that feel like they belong in a sci-fi movie. Think: wild kinetic animations, 3D elements, particle effects, smooth scroll-driven storytelling, glassmorphism, interactive cursors, fluid transitions, and layouts that nobody has seen before.



WHAT WE NEED:

- A single landing page that looks and feels absolutely futuristic and unlike anything 99% of people have ever encountered on the web

- Heavy use of animation libraries (GSAP, Framer Motion, Lenis, etc.)

- 3D elements or WebGL/Three.js integration where it makes sense

- Scroll-triggered animations and micro-interactions everywhere

- Dark, sleek, high-tech aesthetic with creative typography

- Fully responsive and performant despite the heavy visuals

- Clean code that actually works in production



HOW TO APPLY (read carefully, we filter out copy-paste applications):



1. THE VERY FIRST THING in your response must be links to 2-3 live websites YOU personally built that showcase extreme animation and visual creativity. This is the #1 thing we are evaluating. If your portfolio is standard business websites, this is not the right fit. No links = instant skip.



2. Put your favorite color in the subject line of your application. This tells us you actually read the post and are not blasting generic responses.



3. Brief description of the tools/libraries you used on those portfolio projects.



4. Your approach to balancing wild visuals with performance.



Applications that do not follow these steps will not be reviewed.



IDEAL SKILLS:

- GSAP (GreenSock) advanced timeline animations

- Three.js / WebGL / Spline / Rive

- Framer Motion or other React animation libraries

- Lenis or Locomotive Scroll for smooth scrolling

- CSS animations and custom shaders

- Figma to code

- Next.js or modern React frameworks



This is a paid project with potential for ongoing work if the result blows our minds. We want to see your most creative, boundary-pushing work. Show us what you can do when there are no boring constraints.



Budget: Project-based, negotiable depending on skill level and portfolio quality.

title: Shopify Customer Support CSR
description:
The role is for a CSR position, responding to 30+ client enquiries per day via
, Instagram and Meta for an automotive company. 



Here's a longer summary:



We’re a proudly Australian-made automotive brand with a strong reputation for quality and customer experience. We design and manufacture locally, ship worldwide, and continue to grow!



We’re looking for a Customer Experience Lead to be the voice of our brand across
 and social platforms. This is a forward-facing, high-impact role for someone who enjoys connecting with people, helping them get excited about our product, and representing a growing brand with energy and professionalism.



You won’t just be answering tickets, you’ll be helping potential and existing customers when questions arise, guiding them so they can get the most out of our product, and making sure they have experiences that excite them to try it and really enjoy using it. If you love helping people in this way and take pride in representing a growing brand, this role is for you!



Responsibilities:



• Own customer communication across
 and social media, creating a positive and engaging experience



• Manage refunds, disputes, chargebacks, and warranty cases



• Improve and expand our Help Scout knowledge base

You Are:

• Experienced in ecommerce (Shopify)

• Comfortable being the public voice of a brand

Growth Opportunity

What You’ll Do

Content Strategy Execution

Distribution

Performance Tracking

- Content that agents actually want to repost



Requirements

- Ability to make content feel real and trustworthy

- Fast turnaround and organized workflow

- Can think beyond editing and contribute to content direction



Bonus (High Value)

Output Expectations

Compensation

How to Apply

Apply here:

title: STR Operations Assistant
description:
Operations Assistant: Elevate Vacation Homes | U.S.-Based (San Diego, CA)



Read this carefully before applying.



We are a small but growing vacation rental company based in U.S. (San Diego, CA) managing 40+ properties, with 90% located in San Diego, California.

Our team is lean by design:

- 1 CEO

- 1 COO

- 1 CFO

- 2 Operations Assistants



We are now hiring one more Operations Assistant — and this is an important role. We are not hiring in volume. We are hiring one person we can grow with as we scale to 80–100 properties. If you perform well and stay with us, this role will naturally grow into a higher position when we expand.



Important Context (Read This Carefully)

We have been hiring and letting people go over the last 6 months. Not because of pay. Not because of workload. But because we are very selective and have not yet found the right long-term fit.



This role requires:

- Strong communication

- Good judgment

- Ownership

- Reliability under pressure



If you’re confident in your ability, this is a rare opportunity to join early and grow with the company. If you’re unsure, this is not the role for you. This Role Is For One Type of Person Only



You are:

- Fluent in spoken and written English

- Comfortable speaking on the phone with:

- Guests

- Vendors

- Homeowners

- Calm, logical, and clear under pressure

- Able to manage multiple issues at once

- A strong problem solver

- Someone who finishes what they start



You are not:

- A task-only VA

- Someone who avoids calls

- Someone who needs constant direction

- Someone who panics under stress

- Someone who relies on AI to communicate



Communication Expectations (Very Important)

You should expect:

- 5–20 phone calls per day



Daily coordination with:

- Cleaning teams

- Maintenance vendors

- Internal staff



If you are not confident speaking English on calls, do not apply.



What You’ll Actually Be Doing: This is not a customer service role.

Approximate breakdown:

- 10% Guest communication

- 5% Cleaning reminders

- 5% Ordering supplies

- 80% Operations, coordination, and problem-solving



You will function as:

- A project manager

- A communicator between teams

- A problem-solver when things go wrong

- A key operations support role



Work Environment

- Small, fast-moving startup

- High accountability

- Clear expectations

- Real responsibility

- Direct communication with leadership

- Opportunity to grow as the company scales



Compensation & Benefits

- Up to $8.00 USD/hour (based on performance)

- 10 days paid time off per year

- 2 days off per week

- Long-term role with growth potential

- Promotion opportunity as we scale to 80–100 properties



Final Note: We are not looking to hire quickly. We are looking to hire correctly. If you believe your experience, communication skills, and work ethic make you a strong fit — apply. If you are unsure, this role is not for you.



How to Apply



If you believe you are a strong fit and meet all of the requirements above, apply using the link below.





Important:

Applications that are dis-honest, incomplete, generic, or clearly copy-pasted will be ignored. We are only moving forward with candidates who demonstrate strong communication, attention to detail, and professionalism. If you’re confident in your abilities and want to grow with a serious team — apply now.

title: Bookkeeper (QuickBooks Specialist)
description:
Part-Time | Remote | £6 per hour



About the Role

We are recruiting on behalf of a growing UK-based accountancy firm, an 8-figure firm, seeking a detail-oriented and highly accurate Bookkeeper (QuickBooks Specialist) to support their client portfolio.



This is a key role responsible for maintaining clean, compliant, and up-to-date financial records across multiple clients. You will work closely with the Director to ensure bookkeeping is completed to a high standard, deadlines are met, and all records are fully prepared for accountant review.



Note: This is not basic data entry — this role requires ownership of bookkeeping, accuracy, and workflow.



Working Hours

10:00am – 3:00pm UK Time (Tuesday & Thursday)

5:00pm – 10:00pm Philippines Time



Key Responsibilities



Bookkeeping & QuickBooks Management

Maintain accurate, up-to-date records in QuickBooks Online

Post and allocate bank transactions

Manage purchase invoices and expenses

Post sales invoices and allocate receipts

Maintain clean and accurate ledgers (debtors, creditors, nominal)

Perform bank, credit card, and balance sheet reconciliations

Prepare VAT returns and support submissions

Process journal entries (within competence)

Ensure all records are fully supported and audit-ready



Deadlines & Workflow Management

Manage VAT, month-end, and reporting deadlines

Start work early and avoid last-minute deadlines

Request missing information proactively

Keep all work progressing without backlog



Client Communication

Respond to client queries within 1 working day

Communicate clearly and professionally

Keep communication simple and commercially aware

Escalate anything outside your level of expertise



Data Accuracy & Cleanliness

Maintain clean ledgers with no duplication or miscoding

Ensure all accounts are fully reconciled

Clear suspense accounts promptly

Monitor aged debtors and creditors

Regularly review bank feeds and financial data



Escalation & Awareness

Identify and flag unusual transactions

Highlight compliance risks or inconsistencies

Raise cash flow concerns where relevant

Escalate anything unclear or outside your scope



Reporting

Provide weekly updates on:



Work completed

Work in progress

Missing information

Risks or issues



Essential Skills & Experience

Strong experience using QuickBooks Online (non-negotiable)

Proven experience in bookkeeping or accountancy support

Strong understanding of reconciliations, VAT, and ledgers

High level of accuracy and attention to detail

Strong English communication skills

Able to manage workload independently

Organised and process-driven



What We’re Looking For

We want someone who:



Takes full ownership of their work

Keeps financial records clean, accurate, and review-ready

Works proactively (not reactively)

Manages deadlines without being chased

Communicates clearly with both clients and internal team

Spots issues early and raises them



What This Role Isn’t

Not basic data entry

Not leaving reconciliations unfinished

Not allowing backlog to build

Not ignoring errors or inconsistencies



How to Apply:

Use “Vixa Hire” in the subject line of your application.

Send your resume and cover letter.

Include your internet speed test result and a screenshot of your laptop specs.



If you’re a detail-driven QuickBooks specialist who takes pride in clean, accurate bookkeeping, this is a great opportunity to take ownership within a growing UK accountancy firm.

title: Software Engineer (AI / Machine Learning) – Project / Casual Role
description:
AU Hours (AEST/AEDT) | Project / Casual Engagement



About the Role



We’re looking for a skilled Software Engineer with AI/ML experience to join our team on a project or casual basis, supporting Australian-based clients.



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working on cutting-edge AI applications, including LLMs, GenAI, and scalable cloud systems, while collaborating with international teams.



You’ll play a key role in building intelligent, production-ready solutions that drive real business impact.



What You’ll Do



Build and maintain scalable backend systems, APIs, and data pipelines

Integrate AI/ML features such as:

LLM-powered applications

Embeddings & vector search

RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipelines

Deploy and manage applications on AWS, Azure, or GCP

Develop and optimize prompt engineering strategies

Collaborate with product and design teams to turn ideas into solutions

Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code

Participate in code reviews and technical discussions

Help evaluate AI model performance, safety, and reliability

Identify and fix performance issues, bugs, and technical debt



Required Skills & Experience



3+ years experience in software engineering

Strong skills in Python and/or JavaScript (TypeScript preferred)

Hands-on experience with AI/ML in real-world applications

Experience using LLM APIs (OpenAI, Claude, Cohere, etc.)

Familiar with ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, Hugging Face)

Experience building REST APIs / microservices

Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)

Experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, etc.)

title: Hospice QA Coordinator (Home Health)
description:
DO NOT APPLY if you do not meet the qualifications listed below



Position Summary



We are seeking a detail-oriented and service-driven Virtual Hospice Quality Assurance (QA)  to provide after-hours, evening, and weekend support for hospice operations.



This role is primarily responsible for quality assurance, compliance monitoring, and documentation accuracy, while also supporting intake coordination, patient call management, scheduling follow-ups, and EMR documentation.



The ideal candidate has prior hospice or home health experience, strong knowledge of documentation compliance standards, excellent English communication skills, and the ability to remain calm and professional in time-sensitive or sensitive patient situations.



Key Responsibilities

Hospice Quality Assurance (Primary Focus)



• Review and audit patient charts to ensure compliance with hospice regulations and company standards

• Track and follow up on missing, incomplete, or non-compliant documentation

• Ensure timely upload of physician orders, certifications, and required hospice documentation

• Maintain organized QA logs, audit trackers, and electronic records

• Support compliance readiness for internal reviews and external audits

• Flag documentation discrepancies and escalate issues to appropriate leadership



Intake & Documentation Support



• Assist with hospice intake processing and referral coordination

• Upload and track physician orders, certifications, and care plans in EMR

• Monitor and manage endorsement and handoff reports

• Ensure all documentation is complete, accurate, and filed timely



Scheduling & Care Coordination



• Schedule and confirm follow-up visits for hospice patients

• Coordinate with field clinicians, intake teams, and office staff

• Assist in care coordination and communication workflows

• Escalate urgent clinical or operational concerns as needed



Patient & Call Support



• Answer and triage incoming patient, family, and caregiver calls

• Provide compassionate, professional customer service aligned with hospice care standards

• Accurately document all interactions in EMR

• Route concerns promptly and appropriately



Qualifications & Requirements

Required



• Prior healthcare experience (hospice or home health strongly preferred)

• Experience with medical documentation review, QA, or compliance tracking

• Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)

• High attention to detail and strong organizational skills

• Strong computer proficiency (Microsoft Office, spreadsheets, EMR systems)

• Ability to work independently in a remote, fast-paced environment

• Ability to work after-hours, evenings, and weekends (U.S. Pacific Time)

• Reliable high-speed internet and professional remote workspace



Preferred



• Direct Hospice QA or compliance experience

• Experience with Home Health/Hospice EMR systems (e.g., Kinnser/WellSky, Axxess, Hospice MD, Homecare Homebase)

• Clinical background (RN, LVN, Medical Assistant, or allied health)

• Familiarity with hospice regulations and documentation standards



How to Apply



Please submit a short cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and include your answers to the questions below:



Describe your experience in hospice or home health, including your role, duration, and responsibilities.



Do you have experience with quality assurance, chart audits, or compliance tracking? Please explain.



How many years of experience do you have in Hospice or Home Health?



What EMR systems have you used (e.g., Kinnser/WellSky, Axxess, Hospice MD, Homecare Homebase)?



Describe your experience reviewing or managing medical documentation (orders, certifications, care plans, etc.).

title: Short-Form Video Editor (Part-Time ? Full-Time)
description:
We're looking for a reliable short-form video editor to join our team on a part-time basis, with a clear path to full-time as volume increases. You'll be editing vertical (9:16) videos for Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts.

This is a straightforward editing role — all assets will be provided to you. You are assembling, not creating from scratch.



What You'll Be Doing



Editing short-form vertical videos (30–90 seconds) from pre-recorded A-roll (talking head footage) and pre-made B-roll (AI-generated images and video clips)

Cleaning up A-roll: trimming dead air, removing filler words, tightening pacing

Cutting B-roll into the timeline to match the script and visual flow

Adding smooth transitions between A-roll and B-roll

Adding animated captions/subtitles (style reference will be provided)

Adding background music and sound effects where appropriate

Color correcting A-roll for a clean, consistent look

Exporting final videos optimized for each platform



What We Provide



Finished A-roll footage (talking head)

All B-roll assets (AI-generated images and short video clips)

Scripts so you know what's being said and when B-roll should appear

Reference/example videos showing the exact style and pacing we want

Music and sound effect libraries or direction on sourcing



What We're Looking For



Proven experience editing short-form vertical content (show us your work)

Proficiency in CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

Ability to add clean, on-brand animated captions (CapCut auto-captions or similar)

Good sense of pacing and timing — you understand how short-form content flows

Strong attention to detail

Reliable communication and consistent turnaround times

Comfortable receiving feedback and iterating quickly



Nice to Have



Experience editing content in the AI, tech, or creator education space

Familiarity with trending short-form editing styles on TikTok and Instagram

Basic color correction skills

Experience with audio cleanup (noise removal, volume leveling)



Details



Part-time to start (3–5 videos per week), with a path to full-time based on performance and volume

Compensation is per-video and will be discussed after the test edit

Long-term communication and workflow will be managed through Slack



How to Apply



Send your portfolio or links to short-form videos you've edited. We'll select a few candidates for a paid test edit using our actual assets and reference videos before making a final decision.

title: Business Development Assistant
description:
Company: Chasm Opportunities LLC

Founder: David Madden, CEO & Founder

Location: Remote (Philippines-based candidates preferred)

Hours: Part-time, ~4 hours per week to start; long-term with growth potential

Compensation: Negotiable

Chasm Opportunities (CO) is a technology-transition consulting firm supporting ~14 early-stage startup clients in government, defense, energy, and commercial markets. The Principal works directly with each client on an ongoing, fractional-support basis, helping them identify and pursue business development opportunities. We are hiring a part-time, remote BD Assistant to support lead generation, outreach drafting, funding opportunity research, and marketing material improvement across our client portfolio.

About the work

Our clients are very early-stage technology startups commercializing products into government and commercial markets — defense, energy, advanced manufacturing, logistics, and adjacent sectors. They each have a real product or capability but limited BD infrastructure. Your job is to help CO find the right people and organizations for each client, draft the first outreach, monitor relevant funding opportunities, and identify where their marketing materials can be sharpened.

You will work directly with CO's Principal and Executive Assistant, track all activity in Monday CRM, and operate as a disciplined, organized extension of a lean team.

What you'll do

1. Lead research and identification

You will receive general search direction from the Principal (e.g., "Identify relevant POCs within Air Force Training Command who oversee simulation and training systems acquisition") and independently research and build a qualified lead list. This includes: 

• Searching government directories, LinkedIn, agency websites, acquisition portals (e.g., 
, military small business sites), and relevant industry sources.

• Identifying specific individuals: name, title, organization, program office or division,
/phone where available, and a brief rationale for why they are a relevant target.

• Flagging warm-connection opportunities (e.g., mutual contacts, prior engagement) where they exist.

• Entering all leads cleanly into Monday CRM following the established board structure and field conventions.

2. Outreach drafting, tracking, and follow-up management

You will draft outreach
s for the Principal's review and approval before anything is sent, and then maintain clean, current records of all outreach activity in Monday CRM. This includes: 

• Drafting concise, professional, tailored outreach
s — warm introductions, cold outreach to targeted prospects, and follow-up
s.

• Clearly communicating each client's value proposition in plain language, with no jargon or generic pitches.

• Logging each touchpoint 
 sent, response received, follow-up needed) against the correct contact and client account in Monday CRM.

• Providing a short biweekly status summary: what went out, what came back, and what needs attention.

3. Funding opportunity research via RSS feed monitoring

You will build and maintain a structured RSS feed monitoring system to surface relevant grant and funding opportunities for each client. This includes: 

• Developing a tailored keyword set for each client based on their technology domain, market focus, and target funding sources (e.g., SBIR/STTR, DOE, DOD, ARPA-E, commercial grants).

• Monitoring active RSS feeds from relevant agencies, databases, and publications on a regular cadence and flagging any opportunities that match a client's keywords and profile.

• Presenting matched opportunities to the Principal with a brief summary: funding source, opportunity title, relevance to the client, deadline, and a link to the full posting.

• Managing the feed library actively: adding new feeds as new clients are onboarded or new funding sources become relevant, and removing feeds that are no longer applicable as the client portfolio evolves.

• Keeping the feed and keyword library documented and current so it remains an asset that reflects the current portfolio at all times.

4. Marketing material review and improvement suggestions

Each client has some combination of a website, flyers, and/or quad charts — most are early-stage and need polish. You will: 

• Review existing materials for clarity, consistency, and effectiveness in communicating the client's capability and market fit.

• Identify specific, actionable improvements: unclear messaging, missing calls to action, misaligned audience framing, design inconsistencies, or content gaps.

• Provide written feedback in plain language that the client or CO can act on.

• You are not expected to produce final design deliverables — your role is to provide clear, thoughtful critique and recommendations.

What we're looking for

• Experience in B2B or government-facing business development, lead research, or sales support — particularly in defense or government contracting contexts.

• Strong research skills: ability to find the right person in a large government or corporate organization with minimal starting information, and to identify relevant funding opportunities from public sources.

• Excellent written English: you will be drafting outreach on behalf of a senior consultant to government and industry contacts, so professional tone and precision matter greatly.

• Demonstrated experience building and managing RSS feeds or monitoring systems for opportunity tracking, grant research, or competitive intelligence.

• Comfort working in Monday CRM (or a similar CRM); the architecture is already designed — you must be organized and disciplined about keeping records clean and current.

• Ability to give constructive, specific feedback on marketing materials.

• Self-directed and reliable in an async, remote environment.

Engagement details

• Type: Part-time, freelance

• Work style: Fully remote, primarily async with periodic check-ins with the Principal and Executive Assistant.

• Starting scope: ~4 hours per week, with expectation to grow as the role proves out

• Timeline: Long-term engagement for the right candidate

How to apply

Please provide:

1. A brief description of a lead research project you've completed — what the target audience was, how you found leads, and what tools you used.

2. A short sample or description of an outreach
 you drafted for an industry contact on behalf of a technology company, and how you tracked and managed follow-up.

3. Your experience monitoring RSS feeds, grant databases, or opportunity pipelines — what tools you used, how you organized the results, and how you communicated matches to your team or client.

4. Any additional information you want to share to show how your capabilities align with this job posting.

5. Your currently hourly rate and availability.

title: Google Ads media buyer — lead gen
description:
We're looking for a senior Google Ads Media Buyer who can own lead gen accounts end-to-end. No micromanagement. No endless client calls. Just strategy, execution, and scaling. If you treat ad spend like your own money and proactively find growth levers, you'll fit in well.



Why Join Us

Full autonomy: You run the accounts. We trust your judgment.

Growth path: Start part-time, scale to full-time, move into a lead/management role as we grow.



What You'll Do

Manage and scale Google Ads accounts focused on lead generation (setup ? testing ? scaling).

Build and own Search, Performance Max, and Display strategies tuned for CPL and lead quality.

Develop keyword strategies, ad copy frameworks, and landing page hypotheses that drive qualified form fills and calls.

Optimize toward CPL, lead-to-close rate, and blended CAC — not vanity metrics like CTR or impressions.

Structure campaigns for scale (TOFU prospecting, BOFU retargeting, branded vs non-branded separation).

Identify and execute scaling opportunities — don't just maintain spend.



Requirements

3+ years of Google Ads experience with a lead generation focus (not eCommerce).

Proven results in high-intent verticals: finance, insurance, real estate, legal, or similar.

Strong grasp of keyword intent, match type strategy, and Quality Score mechanics.

Experience structuring campaigns around CPL targets with an understanding of downstream lead quality.

Self-directed: you create the plan and execute without hand-holding.

Results-driven: measured on outcomes, not hours worked.



Bonus Points

Experience working with a mortgage broker, financial advisor firm, or lending company is a strong plus. We work with financial services clients and need someone who understands compliance constraints, high-value leads, and long sales cycles.



How to Apply

Apply here:

title: Nurse Content Reviewer & Curriculum Assistant
description:
We are developing a new educational document for nursing students and need a sharp, academically strong nursing student or nurse to critically review and enhance our content. This role is focused on ensuring our material aligns with current nursing curriculum standards, is academically accurate, and is pedagogically sound. You will be working closely with our in-house Nurse and Graphic Designer. This is a project-based, remote contract/internship position.



Key Responsibilities

Curriculum Alignment Check: Review chapters to ensure the content, terminology, and concepts are consistent with the latest nursing curriculum (CMO/CHED standards) and current institutional teachings.



Content Relevance Review: Assess the material for academic accuracy and clarity, pointing out any sections that are confusing, outdated, or poorly explained from a student's perspective.



Restructuring Input: Offer suggestions on how topics are ordered, grouped, and presented to maximize student comprehension and retention.



Collaboration: Provide input to the Nurse and Graphic Designer regarding the effectiveness of planned educational visuals (charts, tables, diagrams) for student learning.



Proofreading: Assist with proofreading and editing for grammatical and technical errors.



What You'll Bring (Required Qualifications)

We are looking for a highly motivated student with excellent academic standing and a strong grasp of fundamental nursing principles.



Academic Status: Currently enrolled as a student in a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program or a nurse.



Academic Excellence: Strong academic records are preferred 



Knowledge: Solid foundational knowledge in core nursing subjects (e.g., Anatomy & Physiology, Fundamentals of Nursing, Medical-Surgical Nursing).



Skills:



Excellent written and verbal communication abilities in English.



Strong critical thinking and attention to detail for identifying errors.



Proficiency with digital documents Canva & Procreate



Self-starter attitude with an ability to manage time effectively.



How to Apply

Please submit your resume/CV, a brief cover letter, and include the following:



Your current year level and university.



A brief description of one subject or topic from your studies that you feel is exceptionally well-structured and why, or one that could be greatly improved.

title: Sales Development Representative (SDR)
description:
Job Title: Sales Development Representative (SDR)

Location: Remote, Philippines

Engagement Type: Full-time contractor

Company: Sprint Labs

Client Account: Linxio



Sprint Labs is hiring a high-energy, results-driven Sales Development Representative to support one of our clients in Australia, an established provider of GPS tracking and fleet management solutions.



This is a full-time remote role for someone in the Philippines who is confident on the phone, comfortable with cold outreach, and hungry to build pipeline. You will be responsible for generating interest from prospective business customers through outbound prospecting across phone,
, and LinkedIn.



This role is not about sitting back and waiting for leads to come in. We want someone who can take ownership of the top of funnel, actively build lists, work leads with persistence, and create qualified opportunities for the sales team.



You will be engaged by Sprint Labs and work closely on the company account, helping drive new business conversations and booked demos.



What you’ll be doing



As the SDR, you will own outbound prospecting activity from start to finish.



Your day-to-day work will include:

 • Prospecting potential clients through cold calling, cold
, and LinkedIn outreach

 • Building and managing prospect lists using tools such as Apollo, Dripify, HubSpot, and other sales and marketing platforms

 • Working from lead lists provided by the business, while also sourcing and identifying your own target accounts and contacts

 • Reaching out to decision-makers and key stakeholders to introduce Linxio’s solution

 • Following up consistently across multiple channels to generate interest and move prospects forward

 • Qualifying prospects to determine fit, interest level, and next steps

 • Booking demos directly into the sales representative’s calendar where appropriate

 • Handing over warm opportunities and qualified lead details to the sales representative for further follow-up

 • Keeping CRM records accurate and up to date

 • Tracking outreach activity, response rates, leads generated, and meetings booked

 • Contributing ideas to improve prospecting strategy, messaging, targeting, and pipeline generation



What success looks like in this role



This is a performance-focused role. We are looking for someone who is proactive, commercially minded, and highly consistent with activity.



Success in this role would look like:

 • High daily outbound activity across calls,
s, and LinkedIn

 • Strong discipline around follow-up and pipeline hygiene

 • Qualified leads being generated consistently

 • Demos booked for the sales team

 • Good judgement in identifying businesses that are a fit for Linxio

 • Professional and confident communication with prospective clients



A strong SDR in this role knows how to keep momentum. Some days that means making a large volume of calls. Other days it means chasing up warm prospects, refining a list, or finding the right contact inside a company that looked cold at first.



Who we’re looking for



You’re probably a good fit if you are someone who enjoys outbound sales and doesn’t get rattled by rejection. You know that pipeline is built through consistent effort, sharp communication, and relentless follow-up.



We’d like to hear from people who have:

 • Experience in an SDR, BDR, lead generation, telemarketing, or outbound sales role

 • Confidence making a high volume of cold calls each day

 • Experience with cold
 outreach and LinkedIn prospecting

 • Familiarity with CRM and prospecting tools such as HubSpot, Apollo, Dripify, or similar platforms

 • Strong written and spoken English

 • A professional and confident phone manner

 • Good organisational skills and attention to detail

 • The ability to work independently from home and stay productive in a remote environment

 • A results-driven attitude and genuine motivation to generate leads and open opportunities



Bonus points if you have

 • Experience selling B2B software or SaaS

 • Experience selling into fleet, logistics, transport, operations, construction, field services, or related industries

 • Experience booking demos for an Account Executive or Sales Representative

 • A strong understanding of how to research accounts and identify decision-makers



Pay and benefits



This role offers:

 • Competitive full-time contractor rate

 • Performance-based bonus for every lead generated

 • Fully remote work from home arrangement

 • Opportunity to work with an established client account

 • Ongoing support, systems, and prospecting tools provided

 • A role with clear activity and performance expectations, where strong performers can stand out quickly



Important note



This is a contractor role, not an employee position. You will be engaged by Sprint Labs and assigned to work on there clients account.



How to apply



Please apply with:

 • Your resume

 • A short note introducing yourself

 • Details of your experience in outbound sales, cold calling, or lead generation

 • Any examples of tools you’ve used, such as HubSpot, Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Dripify, or similar



If you’ve worked in a role where your day involved calling prospects, following up leads, and booking meetings, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Shopify Developer
description:
Wood&Co. is hiring a Shopify Developer (full-stack / hybrid) to support the build of its ecommerce platform.



We are developing a design-led, made-to-order ecommerce experience with structured product configurations and a strong emphasis on presentation quality. This role involves working closely with the founder and project lead in an iterative, hands-on process, helping translate business logic and product structure into a functional Shopify storefront.



Key Responsibilities

     - Develop and customize a Shopify storefront based on defined requirements

     - Set up and organize product data, including attributes, variants, and metafields

     - Implement product option logic and pricing behavior based on defined rules

     - Build and refine product page layouts using custom sections or templates

     - Ensure consistent application of brand guidelines across the site

     - Support product data uploads and ongoing updates

     - Evaluate and integrate third-party apps or custom solutions where needed

     - Ensure the site functions properly across desktop, tablet, and mobile

     - Participate in working sessions and contribute to implementation decisions



Qualifications

     - Solid experience building and customizing Shopify stores (Liquid, themes, sections)

     - Comfortable working as a hybrid developer (handling both development and layout execution)



Experience with:

     - Complex product setups (multiple options, configurations)

     - Pricing adjustments based on selections or conditions

     - Metafields and structured product information

     - Managing product data via CSV or similar workflows

     - Customizing product detail pages beyond default templates



You should be able to:

     - Work with pre-defined systems and translate them into implementation

     - Think through edge cases and suggest practical solutions

     - Maintain clean structure in both code and layout

     - Apply visual guidelines with attention to spacing and hierarchy

     - Communicate clearly and work collaboratively



Engagement Details

     - Hiring ASAP

     - Engagement: full-time or near full-time equivalent

     - Flexible hours, with availability for ongoing coordination

     - Work will be structured around milestones and iterative delivery



Application Instructions

Please submit your application in a Google Drive folder (sent via link). Include the following:

     1. Your CV

     2. A 1–2 minute intro video covering: (a) Your background with Shopify; (b) A project where you handled product configuration or pricing logic; (c) How you approach more complex builds; and (d) Why this role interests you

     3. A Google Doc containing:

     - Links to Shopify stores you’ve worked on, with a brief explanation of your role in each

     - Short answers to: (a) Your experience with product variants or configuration logic; (b) Your experience working with product data (metafields, bulk uploads, etc.); and (c) Your experience customizing product pages

     - Your availability: Hours per week, time zone, and earliest start date

title: Full time AI video creator
description:
Hiring: AI Content Creation VA (Short-Form Video)



We are a social media content company that creates short-form video content for health and wellness brands on Instagram. We use AI tools to generate images, voiceovers, talking head videos, and motion clips, then edit everything together into finished Instagram Reels.



This is a long-term position, not a one-time project.



What you will be doing:



- Using AI tools to generate realistic images of characters (Nano Banana Pro)

- Generating natural sounding voiceovers (ElevenLabs)

- Creating talking head videos (HeyGen)

- Generating motion video clips (Google Veo 3)

- Editing everything together into polished short-form videos (CapCut or your preferred editing software)

- Using Claude AI to write prompts and improve your work quality



You do not need prior experience with these specific tools. We have a full 9-stage onboarding program that trains you on everything step by step. But you do need to be someone who learns fast and pays attention to detail.



What we are looking for:



- Reliable and consistent. You show up every day and communicate if something comes up. This is the number one thing we care about.

- Detail-oriented. The difference between a good video and a bad one comes down to small details.

- Coachable. You take feedback seriously and apply it to your next video.

- Has their own laptop or desktop computer with a stable internet connection.

- Has their own video editing software (CapCut works great).

- Can follow instructions carefully without needing to be told the same thing twice.



Schedule:



Monday through Saturday. Daily check-in at 9:00 AM PHT. All work for the day must be completed and submitted by 9:00 PM PHT.



Pay:



$500 per month.



Onboarding:



Before you start producing content, you will go through our 9-stage onboarding program. Onboarding is unpaid but mandatory and must be completed within 7 days. This deadline is non-negotiable. It is designed to fully prepare you so you can do the job at a high level from day one. If onboarding is not completed within 7 days we will not be moving forward.



To apply:



Do not just send me a message saying you are interested. To be considered for this position you must complete the following test assignment and include it in your application:




  



Applications without the completed assignment will not be reviewed. This is how we filter for people who actually pay attention and follow instructions.



If you have any questions about the role, include them in your message along with your completed assignment.



Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Customer Service Rep with E-commerce Experience
description:
Customer Service Representative



Remote | United States | 25–30 hrs/week | Monday – Friday



**Must have prior experience working with e-commerce brands — experience with a direct response health/supplement brand is strongly preferred.**



---



**ABOUT CLARITY MD**



Clarity MD is a US-based health and wellness company delivering high-quality products and exceptional customer experiences.



**ROLE OVERVIEW**



As our Customer Service Representative, you will be the frontline of communication between Clarity MD, our customers, and our suppliers. You will manage customer inquiries via
, coordinate with suppliers to resolve product issues, handle refund and damaged goods claims, and keep the team informed through regular reporting. This role is 25–30 hours per week, Monday through Friday, ideal for a self-starter who thrives in a fast-paced environment.



**REQUIREMENTS**



- Proven experience in customer service within an e-commerce environment (direct response health/supplement brand preferred)

- Proficiency with
 platforms, CRM tools, and basic reporting (Google Sheets, Commslayer)

- Reliable internet connection and able to work EST timezone



**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**



Customer Communication

- Respond to customer
s promptly and professionally, addressing inquiries, concerns, and complaints

- Maintain a friendly, solution-oriented tone in all communications

- Track and follow up on open customer issues to ensure timely resolution



Supplier Coordination

- Communicate directly with suppliers to escalate and resolve product issues on behalf of customers

- Stay on top of supplier responses — follow up persistently until problems are fully resolved

- Coordinate refunds and replacements for damaged or defective goods

- Ensure Clarity MD is properly refunded or credited for damaged inventory



Reporting

- Prepare and submit end-of-day reports summarizing daily activity, open issues, and resolutions

- Compile end-of-week reports highlighting key metrics, trends, and outstanding items

- Flag recurring issues or patterns to help the team improve processes



Team Collaboration

- Attend a minimum of two 20-minute tea
etings per week

- Contribute updates, insights, and feedback during team check-ins

- Collaborate with tea
mbers to improve the overall customer experience



**WHAT WE OFFER**



- Flexible, remote work schedule (Monday – Friday)

- A supportive and collaborative team environment

- Opportunity to grow with a fast-moving company in the health and wellness space

- Hands-on experience across customer service, supplier management, and operations



**HOW TO APPLY**



If you're a proactive communicator who takes ownership of problems and sees them through to resolution, we'd love to hear from you. Please send your resume and a brief cover letter explaining why you're a great fit for this role.



*Clarity MD is an equal opportunity employer.*

title: Industrial Designer/Engineer - Hard & Soft Goods
description:
POSITION SUMMARY

We are seeking a talented and experienced Product Designer to join our design team. This role will focus on developing both hard goods (kitchenware, home décor, tableware, storage solutions) and soft goods (textiles, bedding, table linens, home accessories) for our seasonal product lines. The ideal candidate is proficient in Adobe Creative Suite, has strong textile and print design skills, and understands the product development process from concept through production.

 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Design & Development:

• Create original designs for hard and soft goods across multiple home product categories

• Develop patterns, prints, and textile designs using Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop

• Design seasonal collections that align with market trends and brand direction

• Produce technical sketches, renderings, and detailed specifications for manufacturing

• Create colorways and maintain brand color standards across product lines

• Develop presentation boards and line sheets using Adobe InDesign

Product Development & Production:

• Manage products from initial concept through final production

• Create comprehensive technical specification packages including materials, construction details, and finishes

• Collaborate with overseas vendors and factories to ensure design integrity

• Review and approve prototypes, samples, and pre-production units

• Provide feedback and revisions to manufacturing partners

• Support quality control processes and address production issues

• Assist with cost analysis and value engineering when needed

Collaboration & Communication:

• Work cross-functionally with merchandising, sourcing, and sales teams

• Present design concepts and seasonal line reviews to internal stakeholders

• Research market trends, competitive products, and consumer preferences

• Attend trade shows and markets as needed

• Maintain organized design files, asset libraries, and documentation

 

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Experience:

• 3-7 years of professional product design experience in hard and soft goods

• Proven track record designing home products, textiles, or related categories

• Experience working with overseas vendors and understanding of manufacturing processes

• Portfolio demonstrating strong design skills across multiple product categories

Technical Skills:

• Expert proficiency in Adobe Illustrator

• Expert proficiency in Adobe Photoshop

• Advanced proficiency in Adobe InDesign

• Strong understanding of textile and print design

• Ability to create camera-ready artwork and production files

• Knowledge of pattern development and repeats

• Technical specification writing experience

Design Capabilities:

• Strong understanding of color theory and trend forecasting

• Excellent visual communication and presentation skills

• Ability to translate concepts into commercially viable products

• Understanding of materials including ceramics, glass, metal, wood, textiles, and finishes

• Knowledge of manufacturing processes for both hard and soft goods

Professional Skills:

• Exceptional attention to detail and organizational abilities

• Strong time management skills with ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously

• Excellent communication and collaboration skills

• Problem-solving mindset with focus on practical solutions

• Adaptable to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment

• Self-motivated with ability to work independently and as part of a team

 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Bachelor's degree in Product Design, Textile Design, Industrial Design, or related field

• Experience in the wholesale/import industry

• Knowledge of product safety standards and compliance requirements

• Proficiency in Adobe After Effects or Premiere

• Experience with CAD software

• Familiarity with retail market and consumer trends

• Trade show and market experience



To apply: Please submit your application through the provided link below:

title: Short-Form Video Editor (Fully Flexible, $3,000-$5,000 / month)
description:
Before applying, complete the following (incomplete applications will not be reviewed):

- Share 3 videos you've edited, not a portfolio page, just 3 specific links. For each one, write one sentence on why you're proud of it.

- Watch this video: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bamjMX35cpjt3qHoT8pEnfRIupRShGto/view?usp=sharing and tell us what you would change about the editing and why.

- Start your application message with the phrase "I'm ready!"



Role Overview



We are looking for a highly creative and results-driven Performance Video Editor to join our team. Your mission will be to create high-performing organic video for Instagram and TikTok that don't look like ads. If you have a knack for creating content that stops the scroll and engages audiences, we want to talk to you.



This is a long-term, fully remote position for an editor who is passionate about the intersection of creativity and data, and is excited by the opportunity to have their work directly impact business results.



What You'll Do:



- Edit raw footage into compelling, fast-paced short-form video tailored for organic TikToks and Instagram Reels.

- Incorporate viral trends, engaging hooks, and retention-focused pacing to maximize performance.

- Add dynamic captions, relevant B-roll, and sound design to create a native viewing experience.

- Collaborate with our marketing team to test new creative concepts and iterate on winning ads to scale campaigns.

- Analyze ad performance to understand what resonates with the audience and apply those insights to future edits.



Who We're Looking For:



- A Viral Content Creator: You have proven experience creating organic short-form videos (TikToks, Reels) that have achieved significant reach and engagement. You understand what makes content shareable and how to capture attention in the first three seconds.

- A Performance-Minded Editor: You are excited by the idea of your creative work driving measurable results. Experience editing direct-response or paid social ads is a major plus.

- CapCut or Premiere Pro Whiz: You are highly proficient in a fast-paced editing workflow using modern tools.

- Excellent Communicator: You have strong English skills and are reliable, detail-oriented, and can work independently.

- Available & Ready: You have a perfect internet connection and are available to dedicate 3-4 hours daily to this role.

- This is a part-time remote role requiring approximately 24 hours per week (around 4 hours per day, Monday to Saturday), with the potential to grow into a full-time position based on performance.



Compensation & Growth: Earn Based on Performance



We believe in rewarding top talent for outstanding results. This role offers a unique compensation structure designed to give you significant earning potential.



- Base Rate: You will start with a competitive base rate of $10 per hour (596 PHP / hour)

- Performance Bonuses: On top of your hourly rate, you will earn substantial monthly bonuses for creating viral videos and "winning" ads that meet or exceed performance targets. Our bonus structure is uncapped.

- Earning Potential: Our top-performing editors consistently earn $3,000 - $5,000+ per month by creating ads that convert.



This is not a gig. It is a long-term partnership. Most of our editors have been with us for over a year, and we are committed to investing in your growth and success.



Why Join Our Team?



- Fully Remote: Work from anywhere in the world.

- Flexible Schedule: Manage your own hours and workflow.

- Direct Impact: See your creative work translate directly into business success and your own earnings.

- Growth & Stability: Join a team that values long-term relationships and provides ongoing training and opportunities.

title: Video Editor for High-Performance Meta Ads
description:
We're a growing ecommerce brand and looking to bring on new editors to expand. 



What we need is more talented editors who can execute at pace while exploring fresh angles.



If you care about performance data, creative strategy, and actually seeing the impact of your work - keep reading.



What You'll Be Doing:



1. Editing 10–15 direct-response video ads per week from detailed creative briefs

2. Building new variations of proven winners to help us scale what's already working

3. Working directly alongside our creative strategists - daily feedback, daily collaboration

4. Researching competitor ads and market trends so you understand what's performing

5. Using industry-leading tools — ElevenLabs, HeyGen, Higgsfield, VEO, Midjourney, and more

What We're Focused On:

Who We're Looking For

2. People who move fast, communicate clearly, and take ownership of their output

3. Someone passionate about the craft who puts genuine effort into every piece

Growth Path:

If you want to develop into a marketer and not just stay behind the timeline, this is the place to do it.

title: Graphic Designer & Video Editor (with Social Media Skills)
description:
We’re Firestarter—a fast-growing international creative company helping entrepreneurs and experts build high-caliber brands. Through our creative agency, coaching programs, and digital platforms, we combine strategy, design, and tech to empower leaders to step into their next-level vision. We’re hiring a full-time Graphic & Video Designer to join our team!



As our Graphic & Video Designer, you’ll work closely with our CEO/Creative Director and our Design & Brand Experience Lead to support both company brand presence and client projects. We have a lot of internal graphic design and other creative production needs sitting in the pipeline, and are excited for you to jump in and start working closely with our team. 



Responsibilities

- Graphic Design & Animation: Create professional graphics and branded assets using Canva, Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign), and other tools, including animated graphics. 

- Video Editing: Edit engaging videos for social media, podcasts, and YouTube (Premiere Pro, CapCut)

- Podcast Editing: Edit audio and video for company podcast

- Social Media Branding: Develop brand-aligned social assets, templates, and graphics.

- Website Updates: Keep websites fresh by interfacing with our development team to add new testimonials, client stories, and assets.

- Figma: Use for collaboration, mockups, and design workflows.



Preferred (but not required): GoHighLevel & funnel design experience for alignment with our digital platform work.



You’re a Great Fit If You…

 • Are highly creative with a sharp eye for branding and visual storytelling.

 • Have proven experience in both design and video editing.

 • Are detail-oriented, organized, and thrive in a remote, collaborative environment.

 • Can juggle priorities and move efficiently through varied daily, monthly, and weekly creative team needs. 

 • Are proactive, resourceful, a good team player and communicator, and willing to learn new tools.



Requirements

 • Strong portfolio showcasing graphic design and video editing work.

 • Proven experience with: Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, Premiere Pro, CapCut, Figma.

 • Reliable internet connection and a professional home office setup. Our team communicates via Slack & Zoom. 

 • Excellent written and spoken English communication skills. Comfortable on camera meeting with team & clients. 

 • Ability to work US hours - ideally Eastern Time 9am-5pm ET



Bonus Skills (Nice to Have)

 • GoHighLevel platform familiarity (we run our company on GoHighLevel and have a white label SaaS for clients)

 • Funnel design experience.



What We Offer

 • Full-time, long-term role with a fast-growing international creative company.

 • Work-from-home flexibility.

 • Professional growth opportunities, including optional GoHighLevel certification training.

 • A supportive, collaborative team environment where your creativity and ideas are valued.

title: Meta Ad Video Editor for SaaS & E-commerce (After Effects Required)
description:
MUST have proven experience with Meta ads for SaaS and/or e-commerce.

MUST be strong in Adobe After Effects.

VIDEO ONLY — this role is not for static designers.



This is not a generic video editing role.

This is not YouTube-style editing.

This is not for static ad design.



We are looking for a performance-focused video editor who knows how to create high-converting video ads for Meta. Will be on project basis.



A big part of this role is editing software demo ads, UI walkthroughs, feature callout videos, UGC-style ads, and direct-response video creatives for SaaS and e-commerce brands.



You must know how to make software look clean, modern, premium, and easy to understand, and how to make e-commerce products look visually compelling in video format.



What you’ll do

Edit short-form video ads for Meta

title: MEP Estimator - Federal Government Contracts (SAM.gov)
description:
About the Role

ARC Electric is a California-based electrical and HVAC contractor growing fast in the federal government contracting space. We bid mechanical, electrical, and plumbing construction contracts through 
 and need an estimator who can handle all three trades under one roof.

This is not a commercial estimating job. Federal procurement has its own language, its own forms, and its own rules. You need to already know how it works. If you've never priced a Davis-Bacon job or filled out an SF-1442, keep scrolling.



What You'll Actually Be Doing

You own the estimate from the 
nt the solicitation drops to the 
nt the bid goes out the door.

Solicitation Analysis



Pull and review federal solicitations from 
 covering mechanical, electrical, and plumbing scope

Read everything: Statement of Work, drawings, specs, amendments, wage determinations, FAR and DFARS clauses

Identify go/no-go issues fast: set-aside eligibility, out-of-state licensing gaps, bonding thresholds, tight deadlines, mandatory site visits

Track every amendment and make sure the final bid reflects all changes before submission



Estimating and Pricing



Build detailed cost estimates covering all three MEP trades: mechanical (HVAC, piping, controls), electrical (power, lighting, distribution, low voltage), and plumbing (domestic water, sanitary, gas)

Break out labor, material, equipment, subcontractor costs, overhead, and bond premiums line by line

Price prevailing wage labor correctly using Davis-Bacon wage determinations pulled by county and state for every applicable trade classification: electricians, HVAC mechanics, plumbers, pipefitters

Build bid schedules and unit price breakdowns formatted exactly the way each solicitation requires

Account for mobilization, insurance, bonding, permits, and any state-specific cost factors



Proposal Development



Complete SF-1442, SF-24, SF-25, SF-25A, and any agency-specific attachments correctly and completely

Assemble Technical Volumes, qualifications packages, and past performance write-ups that actually answer what the solicitation asks for

Coordinate bid bonds with surety and insurance certificates with broker, both timed to hit the submission deadline

Submit through whatever channel the solicitation requires: 
, agency portal,
, or hard copy



Subcontractor Coordination



Identify locally licensed subs for mechanical, electrical, and plumbing work in states where our California licenses have no authority

Build complete sub bid packages with defined scope, applicable drawings and specs, prevailing wage obligations, insurance requirements, and hard internal deadlines

Vet every sub bid for licensing, insurance coverage, Davis-Bacon acknowledgment, and scope completeness

Hold subs to an internal deadline at least 5 to 7 days before the government due date, no exceptions





What You Need to Have

Non-negotiable:



3 or more years estimating all three MEP trades on federal government contracts

Direct experience reading and responding to 
 solicitations start to finish

Solid command of FAR and DFARS contract requirements as they apply to construction

Working knowledge of the Davis-Bacon Act, how to read a wage determination, and how to apply the right labor classifications across mechanical, electrical, and plumbing trades

Experience completing federal bid forms: SF-1442, SF-24, SF-25, SF-25A

Understands bonding: bid bond, payment bond, performance bond, and when each one is triggered

Ability to read construction drawings and full MEP specifications across all three disciplines

Knows the difference between a mandatory requirement and a recommendation in a solicitation, and never mixes them up



Strongly preferred:



Familiarity with UFC, NEC, NFPA, ASHRAE, and IPC standards as they apply to federal facilities construction

Experience working across multiple states and understanding contractor licensing requirements by trade

Familiarity with WAWF or IPP for post-award invoicing setup

Knowledge of small business set-aside types: total small business, SDVOSB, HUBZone, and 8(a)

Experience coordinating multi-trade subcontractor packages on federal jobs





Who You Are

You're organized, detail-obsessed, and you do not miss deadlines. Federal bids close on time whether you're ready or not, and you plan accordingly. You read the solicitation first and ask questions second. You flag problems before they become bid-day fires.

You can jump between a plumbing takeoff, a wage determination, and an SF-1442 without losing your place. You communicate clearly, work independently, and you're not the type to submit a bid with a gap in scope coverage and hope nobody notices.

title: Meta Media Buyer (A-Player Only) – DTC Health Brand (DE Market)
description:
Meta Media Buyer (A-Player Only) – DTC Health Brand (DE Market)



About Us

We’re a fast-growing DTC health brand operating at a high-performance level in the German market. We move fast, we value excellence, and we only work with people who can deliver top-tier output consistently. If you’re an A-player who has already proven yourself in high-growth performance marketing environments, this is where you’ll feel at home.



Role Overview

We’re looking for an exceptional Meta Media Buyer who understands what world-class performance marketing looks like. Someone who can manage both whitelisting and brand-page buying with precision, maintain a clean structure, scale budgets efficiently, and make fast, data-driven decisions.



You should know how to analyze what works, what doesn’t, and why — and communicate this clearly. We want someone who doesn’t just push buttons, but contributes insights, strategic thinking, and creative feedback that moves performance forward.



What You’ll Do



Manage, structure, and scale Meta Ads campaigns (brand page & whitelisting) for the German market



Maintain clean and logical account structures that allow fast scaling and clear overview



Optimize campaigns daily based on KPIs, real-time results, and creative performance



Identify winners early and kill losers fast — with clear reasoning behind each decision



Work closely with our creative team to provide feedback on what worked, what didn’t, and what we should test next



Ensure fast adaptation to performance swings, product pushes, and new campaign angles



Contribute ideas — not just execution — and push the performance standards forward



What We’re Looking For



Proven experience managing Meta Ads for successful DTC brands (please share examples)



Strong track record scaling budgets profitably while maintaining efficiency



Deep understanding of Meta’s performance mechanics, creative testing, and signal quality



Ability to think structurally, act quickly, and maintain a clean, high-level overview at all times



Experience with whitelisting workflows and influencer/creator accounts



Data-driven mindset with the ability to explain what’s working — and what’s not — clearly and directly



If this sounds like you, apply now. If you're a good fit, we’ll schedule a quick call to get to know each other.
